confirmed.title: RichText image toolbar button visible face hardcodes English "Img"
confirmed.detail: The image toolbar button spec uses `render: () => <>Img</>`, so the VISIBLE button face is the English abbreviation "Img". At line 428 the toolbar renders `{spec.render() ?? label}`, meaning when `render()` returns a node it overrides the localized `label`. Every other button either uses a language-neutral mnemonic glyph (B, I, U, S, H1, •, 1., “, <>) or — like the `link` button (line 204, `render: () => null`) — intentionally returns null so the localized label (from t('arqel.fields_advanced.richtext_link', ...)) becomes the visible face. The `image` button does NOT: its `labelKey` 'richtext_image' is already translated (en 'Image', pt_BR 'Imagem' — confirmed in packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/arqel.php:336/333) and used for the aria-label at line 410/418, but a pt_BR/es/fr user still SEES the English text "Img" on the button while the aria-label reads "Imagem". Fix: change line 215 to `render: () => null` (mirroring the `link` button) so the already-localized `richtext_image` label renders as the visible face, keeping visible text and aria-label consistent across locales. This is the same visible-vs-aria mismatch class fixed for the Link/Edit/Preview/Close/Back faces in prior rounds, but the image button was missed because it supplied a glyph-like English literal rather than null.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real and novel. At packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx:215 the image button spec uses `render: () => <>Img</>`. The toolbar renders `{spec.render() ?? label}` (line 428), so a returned node overrides the already-localized `label` (line 410, key 'richtext_image'). Translations exist and differ from the English literal: en 'Image' / pt_BR 'Imagem' (packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/arqel.php:336/333), and the aria-label/title at lines 418/425 correctly use the localized label. Result: a pt_BR/es/fr user SEES "Img" on the button while the aria-label reads "Imagem" — a visible-text-vs-aria mismatch, user-facing. Unlike the `link` button (line 204, `render: () => null`, with a comment block lines 201-203 documenting the pattern), the image button supplies an English literal instead of null, so the localized label never becomes the visible face. Not vendored — fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the ledger's RichTextInput entries cover aria-label/title (line 1121), image-disabled title (1127), placeholder (1133), max-length (989), char counter (995), and the *Link* glyph visible-face (round 12, line 201) — but the image button's visible "Img" face is the same defect class on a *different* button that round 12 missed. Fix: change line 215 to `render: () => null` mirroring the link button.

confirmed.title: VersionHistoryController Versionable-trait 422 message hardcoded English (sibling lines localize)
confirmed.detail: The 422 JSON response returned when a model lacks the Versionable trait hardcodes 'message' => sprintf('Model [%s] does not use the Versionable trait', $modelClass) in English, while the immediately-following sibling responses in the same method (the 403 at the forbidden branch) correctly use $this->message('arqel::messages.versioning.forbidden', 'Forbidden'). This message is delivered to the version-history client/UI. Framework-hardcoded; should use $this->message('arqel::messages.versioning.not_versionable', ...) with a :model placeholder. The ledger covers VersionHistoryController lines 49/60/64 and the VersionRestoreController equivalent (line 54) but NOT this VersionHistoryController:83 trait-check message.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/versioning/src/Http/Controllers/VersionHistoryController.php:83. The 422 response hardcodes 'message' => sprintf('Model [%s] does not use the Versionable trait', $modelClass) in English, while every sibling response in the same __invoke method localizes via $this->message(...): line 49/53 (registry_not_bound), 60 (resource_not_registered), 68 (resource_invalid), and crucially line 88 (the forbidden 403) uses $this->message('arqel::messages.versioning.forbidden', 'Forbidden'). The message() helper exists (lines 164-173) and is clearly the framework convention, so line 83 is a genuine i18n gap. User-facing: this JSON is returned to the version-history client/UI. Not vendored: framework PHP in packages/versioning, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Ledger check: VersionHistoryController appears at lines 49 (round 9), 60 and 64 (round 11); VersionRestoreController equivalent trait-message at line 54 (round 9). NONE of these is line 83 — the trait-check 422 here is distinct and novel. Reproduces, user-facing, not vendored, not in R1-R13 ledger.

confirmed.title: Publisher average-rating stat renders with locale-insensitive decimal separator (toFixed)
confirmed.suggested_key: n/a (app-level Intl.NumberFormat, not a trans() key)
confirmed.detail: The publisher 'Rating médio' stat is rendered as `{stats.avg_rating.toFixed(1)} ★`. `Number.prototype.toFixed` always emits a U+002E '.' as the decimal separator regardless of the active panel locale, so a rating of 4.5 shows as '4.5 ★' even under pt-BR / pt-PT / de-DE / fr-FR where the convention is a comma ('4,5'). This is inconsistent with the sibling 'Downloads' stat on the same screen, which already routes through the locale-aware `formatCompact(stats.total_downloads, locale)` helper. The component already imports `useActiveLocale` and binds `const locale = useActiveLocale()` on line 13, so the locale is in scope and unused for this number. The avg_rating value arrives as a raw float from the server (packages/marketplace/src/Models/Publisher.php:129 `round($avgRating, 2)`), so the display formatting is the client's responsibility and the float itself is correct — the bug is purely the hardcoded-'.' formatting on the client. Repro: set the panel/Inertia i18n locale to pt_BR (so `useActiveLocale()` returns 'pt-BR'), open a publisher profile whose `stats.avg_rating` is 4.5, and observe the stat reads '4.5 ★' instead of the locale-correct '4,5 ★'. Suggested fix: format via `new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, { minimumFractionDigits: 1, maximumFractionDigits: 1 }).format(stats.avg_rating)` (or add a small `formatDecimal(value, locale, digits)` helper to apps/marketplace/resources/js/lib/format.ts alongside formatCompact/formatCurrency and call it here).
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed in source: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/apps/marketplace/resources/js/Pages/Marketplace/PublisherProfile.tsx:142 renders the 'Rating médio' stat as `{stats.avg_rating.toFixed(1)} ★`. Number.prototype.toFixed always emits a U+002E '.' decimal separator regardless of locale, so a 4.5 rating shows '4.5 ★' under pt-BR/de-DE/fr-FR where '4,5' is correct. The component binds `const locale = useActiveLocale()` (line 13) which is in scope but unused for this number, and the sibling Downloads stat (line 136) already routes through the locale-aware `formatCompact(stats.total_downloads, locale)` — confirming the inconsistency. The avg_rating arrives as a raw float (Publisher.php round($avgRating,2)), so client formatting is the display responsibility. format.ts has formatCurrency/formatCompact (both Intl-based) but no formatDecimal, so this path genuinely bypasses locale. User-facing and reproduces.

Not a duplicate: the ledger's only PublisherProfile.tsx entry (round 11, line 11) is titled 'PublisherProfile total-downloads uses hardcoded English compact suffixes (M/k)' — that is the Downloads stat (now fixed via formatCompact on line 136), a different line and different value. The round-11 locale-format entries (Checkout.tsx, CompareTable.tsx price/date, PluginCard.tsx install-count) cover money/compact/date formatting; none address the avg_rating toFixed decimal separator. Distinct defect.

Not vendored: lives in apps/marketplace/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.
